_comment: Source of truth for the /modules page. The module name/description/category fields are taken verbatim from each WinSentinel.Core/Audits/*Audit.cs module's Name/Category/Description properties in the public repo (sauravbhattacharya001/WinSentinel). When the CLI adds or renames an audit module, update this file and run: node scripts/build-modules.mjs

groups.title: Identity & access
groups.blurb: Who can log in, who is an admin, and how credentials are protected.

modules.name: Account Audit
modules.description: Checks local user accounts, admin membership, password policies, and guest account status.

modules.name: Identity & Credential Audit
modules.description: Audits local admin sprawl, stale accounts, password-never-expires flags, LAPS deployment status, cached credential exposure, LSA Protection (RunAsPPL), and Credential Guard.

modules.name: Credential Exposure Audit
modules.description: Scans for stored credentials, exposed secrets, and credential storage misconfigurations.

modules.name: Group Policy Security Audit
modules.description: Checks security-relevant Group Policy settings including account lockout, NTLM restrictions, audit policy, credential protection, SMB signing, and application whitelisting configuration.

groups.title: System hardening
groups.blurb: Core OS posture: the settings attackers rely on you leaving at default.

modules.name: System Audit
modules.description: Checks OS version, Secure Boot, BitLocker, UAC level, and RDP configuration.

modules.name: Registry Security Audit
modules.description: Checks registry-based security policies including UAC, Remote Desktop, credential storage, LSASS protection, scripting hosts, and persistence mechanisms.

modules.name: Defender Audit
modules.description: Checks Windows Defender status, real-time protection, and antivirus definition freshness.

modules.name: Update Audit
modules.description: Checks Windows Update status, pending updates, and last install date.

modules.name: Firewall Audit
modules.description: Checks Windows Firewall profile states (Domain/Private/Public), the default inbound policy, the inbound allow-rule surface, and over-permissive wide-open inbound rules that let any program accept connections from any address on any port.

groups.title: Network & remote access
groups.blurb: Your exposed attack surface: open ports, shares, and remote entry points.

modules.name: Network Audit
modules.description: Checks open ports, listening services, SMB/RDP exposure, IPv6, Wi-Fi security, network profile, LLMNR/NetBIOS/WPAD/mDNS, ICMP redirect acceptance, and ARP anomalies.

modules.name: DNS Security Audit
modules.description: Checks DNS server configuration, DNS-over-HTTPS status, LLMNR/NetBIOS exposure, hosts file integrity, and cache settings.

modules.name: SMB & Network Share Security Audit
modules.description: Checks SMB protocol versions, server- and client-side signing enforcement (NTLM relay exposure), share permissions, null session access, encryption status, and hidden share exposure.

modules.name: Remote Access Security Audit
modules.description: Checks RDP settings (NLA, encryption, TLS layer, device redirection for drives/clipboard/printers/COM-LPT/Plug-and-Play, session shadowing, and password prompt on connect), SSH exposure, third-party remote tools, WinRM hardening (unencrypted traffic, Basic auth, CredSSP credential delegation, wildcard TrustedHosts, unrestricted listeners), Remote Registry, and Remote Assistance for security risks.

modules.name: WiFi Security Audit
modules.description: Checks saved WiFi profiles for weak encryption, auto-connect risks, password exposure, MAC randomization, and network privacy settings.

modules.name: Bluetooth Security Audit
modules.description: Checks Bluetooth radio state, discoverability, paired device trust, exposed services, authentication settings, and legacy pairing risks.

groups.title: Applications & processes
groups.blurb: What is running, what loaded it, and whether any of it is suspicious.

modules.name: Process Audit
modules.description: Checks running processes for unsigned executables, suspicious locations, and known risks.

modules.name: Process Lineage Audit
modules.description: Analyzes parent-child process relationships to detect suspicious execution chains, LOLBin abuse, and living-off-the-land techniques.

modules.name: Windows Service Security Audit
modules.description: Checks Windows services for unquoted paths, excessive privileges, suspicious binaries, disabled security services, and configuration risks.

modules.name: Scheduled Task Security Audit
modules.description: Checks scheduled tasks for suspicious executables, elevated privileges, persistence mechanisms, encoded commands, and missing binaries.

modules.name: Startup Audit
modules.description: Checks startup items, scheduled tasks, and registry run keys for persistence mechanisms.

modules.name: Software Inventory Audit
modules.description: Scans installed programs for unsigned executables, outdated software, suspicious install locations, and potentially unwanted programs.

modules.name: App Security Audit
modules.description: Detects outdated, end-of-life, and insecure software by scanning installed programs against known-safe minimum versions, flagging EOL products, suspicious installs, and duplicate x86/x64 installations.

modules.name: Browser Audit
modules.description: Checks installed browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox), versions, extensions, saved passwords, auto-update, Safe Browsing, SmartScreen, and security settings.

modules.name: Driver Security Audit
modules.description: Checks loaded drivers for unsigned binaries, known vulnerable driver hashes (BYOVD), suspicious load paths, revoked certificates, and driver age risks.

modules.name: PowerShell Security Audit
modules.description: Checks PowerShell execution policy, script block/module logging, transcription, language mode, the legacy v2 engine, AMSI status (including the AmsiEnable=0 registry kill switch), profile.ps1 tampering, and WinRM remoting exposure including unencrypted (plaintext HTTP) transport.

groups.title: Data protection
groups.blurb: Encryption at rest, certificate hygiene, backups, and removable media.

modules.name: Encryption Audit
modules.description: Checks BitLocker status, TPM availability, EFS usage, certificate store health, TLS/SSL configuration, Credential Guard, DPAPI protection, and Kernel DMA Protection (blocks drive-by DMA / evil-maid attacks from malicious PCIe / Thunderbolt / USB4 peripherals), and Diffie-Hellman key-exchange strength (flags weak <2048-bit DH primes vulnerable to Logjam).

modules.name: Certificate Audit
modules.description: Checks Windows certificate stores for expired, weak, or untrusted certificates.

modules.name: Backup Security Audit
modules.description: Checks Volume Shadow Copy, System Restore, File History, backup recency, and ransomware resilience posture.

modules.name: USB & Removable Media Audit
modules.description: Checks USB device history, autorun/autoplay settings, BitLocker-to-Go coverage, USB write-protect policies, and WPD/MTP portable-device restrictions (phones, cameras, media players that transfer files outside USBSTOR) for removable storage.

modules.name: Privacy Audit
modules.description: Checks telemetry level, location tracking, advertising ID, diagnostic data, clipboard sync, and activity history.

groups.title: Detection & forensics
groups.blurb: Evidence of compromise and the virtualization layers attackers hide in.

modules.name: Event Log Analysis
modules.description: Analyzes Windows Event Logs for failed logins, successful remote logons from external IPs (exposed RDP/network), account lockouts, privilege escalation, suspicious PowerShell, audit policy gaps, service installations, log tampering, and Defender events.

modules.name: Virtualization Security Audit
modules.description: Checks Hyper-V, WSL, Windows Sandbox, Docker, and virtualization-based security features for misconfigurations and exposure risks.

modules.name: Environment Variable Security Audit
modules.description: Checks PATH hijacking risks, secrets in environment variables, proxy configuration, and dangerous PATHEXT/TEMP settings.
